title: Level 923
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:}} {{Transparent Theme 2}}{{Blurbackground|file=Level 923.webp|opacity=0.1}}
[[File:1130-2.gif|2000px]]
[[File:Men's Bathroom 923.png|center|frameless|409x409px]] {{Underline|FFFFFF}}{{Underline|FFFFFF}} Level 923
The Cold Restroom {{Underline|FFFFFF}}{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
''Recovered from a notebook found near Stairwell C''
I was staying close to the lights. Everyone does eventually. The darkness below the balconies isn't normal darkness. It's different. Heavy. Like it wants something. The lights made me feel safer. Not safe. Just safer. I had been walking for what I guessed was two days when I reached the staircase. At first I was excited. Stairs meant change. Maybe an exit. Maybe supplies. Maybe people. The staircase descended into the blackness beneath the balcony. There were no lights below the first landing. Only darkness. I should have left. Instead, I counted the steps. Twenty-four. Twenty-five. Twenty-six. The staircase should have reached the next floor. It didn't. The darkness continued. I kept descending. Thirty. Forty. Fifty. Sixty. The orange light above me became a distant glow. Eventually I couldn't see the balcony anymore. Only the stairs beneath my feet. Then I noticed another set of footsteps. Not sounds. Footprints. Dusty footprints. Someone had used the stairs recently. I followed them downward. For hours. The footprints never stopped. Neither did the staircase. Eventually I reached a landing. The first landing since I started descending. A single light flickered overhead. Under it sat a wooden chair. Nothing else. Just a chair. On the chair was a folded piece of paper.
KEEP GOING.
YOU ARE ALMOST THERE.
I don't know why I listened. Maybe because it was proof someone else had been here. Maybe because I wanted to believe there was an end. So I continued downward. The footprints continued too. Always a few steps ahead of me. Always fresh. As though they were being made while I watched. After several more hours, I finally saw another light. Orange. Warm. Familiar. I hurried toward it. Relief flooded through me. The staircase ended at another balcony. The same balcony. The same railing. The same door. The same light fixture. The same endless darkness beyond the edge. At first I thought I had somehow returned to where I started. Then I looked down. There were footprints in the dust. My footprints. Leading from the staircase to where I was standing. As if I had already arrived here before. As if I had already walked this floor. I checked the door. Inside was an empty room. On the desk sat a notebook. My notebook. The notebook you're reading now. Every page was filled. Every word was written in my handwriting. Including this sentence. Including the next one. And the one after that. I turned to the final page. There was only one line.
Don't go down the stairs again.
I looked up from the notebook. The staircase door was open. I don't remember opening it. From somewhere below, deep within the darkness, I heard footsteps climbing upward. Slowly. Patiently. One floor closer. Every few minutes. One more floor. One more floor. One more floor.{{Underline|FFFFFF}} ''The remainder of the notebook is blank.'' {{Collapse|num=923|initial=But what is next?|body= |final= '''Level 923''' is a vast network of outdoor corridors, balconies, stairwells, and landings lit by dull orange lights. The design resembles that of a hotel, apartment complex, or beach promenade, although no entire structure has ever been located. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|width=375px|file=Level 923.webp|text= }} Every walkway overlooks an infinite black vacuum, making it hard to distinguish whether the level is suspended above a vast area or just surrounded by unnatural darkness. Despite gazing outdoors, no sky, stars, weather, or celestial bodies are visible from any point on the level. The air stays still and somewhat warm, and practically every visitor describes a lasting sensation of solitude. Hallways frequently loop back on themselves, staircases might rise for hours without reaching a new floor, and doors seldom lead to predictable destinations. Most doors open into modest utility rooms, vacant flats, or maintenance closets; however, some have been reported to open into whole separate areas of the floor. Wanderers regularly describe seeing distant balconies lighted by orange lights in the darkness, but all attempts to approach these structures fail. Mapping efforts have been generally futile, with portions of the level shifting location when unobserved. Entities are uncommon on '''Level 923'''; but wanderers have reported hearing distant footsteps ascending stairwells from beneath the darkness. The origins of these noises have never been discovered. Those who seek to investigate frequently describe the footfall as moving further away, regardless of pace or direction. More disturbing are tales of figures standing motionless on distant balconies, partially obscured by orange illumination. While no verifiable sightings have happened, witnesses all agree that the figures vanish anytime they are directly viewed. '''Level 923''' should not be explored for a lengthy period of time because of its confusing and psychological consequences. One may enter this level by going 2000 steps in [[Level 922]], noclipping anywhere in [[The Backrooms]] (rarely), and doors labeled "Restroom" in any indoor levels may transport one here to this level. Ways to exit are by entering a house with no lights, which will send one to [[Level 6]], going far enough in the darkness will transport one to [[Level 9]], but if they don't have any light source, it will send one to [[Level 11]], if they have one. Lastly, getting near a street lamp makes one noclip through the floor and transport to a random safe level. }}
